paper_metadata.title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce a framework that abstracts Reinforcement Learning (RL) as a sequence modeling problem. This allows us to draw upon the simplicity and scalability of the Transformer architecture, and associated advances in language modeling such as GPT-x and BERT. In particular, we present Decision Transformer, an architecture that casts the problem of RL as conditional sequence modeling. Unlike prior approaches to RL that fit value functions or compute policy gradients, Decision Transformer simply outputs the optimal actions by leveraging a causally masked Transformer. By conditioning an autoregressive model on the desired return (reward), past states, and actions, our Decision Transformer model can generate future actions that achieve the desired return. Despite its simplicity, Decision Transformer matches or exceeds the performance of state-of-the-art model-free offline RL baselines on Atari, OpenAI Gym, and Key-to-Door tasks.

extraction_data.innovation: Decision Transformer frames reinforcement learning as conditional sequence modeling by using a causally masked Transformer to generate optimal actions based on desired return, past states, and actions. This approach bypasses traditional value functions and policy gradients, enabling direct policy optimization via autoregressive generation.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Offline reinforcement learning datasets (Atari, OpenAI Gym, Key-to-Door)
extraction_data.application: Offline reinforcement learning, policy generation, long-term credit assignment, decision making in sequential environments
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Conditional sequence modeling for policy generation
extraction_data.extension: Uses a reward-to-go signal as a conditioning input to guide action prediction, enabling effective long-horizon decision-making.
